QF.id: IV Fixed Income Instruments and Bond Markets_44

question.text: According to the workbook, the Indian G-Sec market segments include all EXCEPT:

explanation.text:
**The Logic:** The Indian debt market has three segments: (a) Government debt (G-Secs, T-Bills, SDLs), (b) PSU and bank bonds, and (c) Private corporate bonds. Private corporate bonds are a separate segment, not part of the G-Sec market.
**The Trap:** SDLs and special securities are both part of the government securities market. Private corporate bonds are part of the broader debt market but not the G-Sec market.
**Cross-Exam Context:** Understanding market segmentation is tested in Series IV and helps in understanding which instruments serve as underlyings for interest rate derivatives.
**Distributor's Pro-Tip:** G-Secs trade primarily on NDS-OM, while corporate bonds trade on exchange platforms (NSE/BSE). Different platforms mean different liquidity profiles.
